{{Medal|3rd|[[2023 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2023 New Caledonia]]|}}
}}
The '''Vanuatu men's national football team''' ({{langx|fr|Équipe du Vanuatu de football}}) represents [[Vanuatu]] in men's international [[Association football|football]] competitions. The team is governed by the [[Vanuatu Football Federation]], which is currently a member of [[FIFA]] and the [[Oceania Football Confederation]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://khelnow.com/football/2023-06-indian-football-hero-intercontinental-cup-rival-watch-vanuatu|title=Hero Intercontinental Cup Rival Watch: Vanuatu|website=khelnow.com|publisher=Khel Now India|date=4 June 2023|first=Uttiyo|last=Sarkar|access-date=15 June 2023|archive-date=4 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230604190649/https://khelnow.com/football/2023-06-indian-football-hero-intercontinental-cup-rival-watch-vanuatu}}</ref>

==History==

=== Early years (1951–2022) ===
It was known as the '''New Hebrides''' until the [[New Hebrides]] became Republic of Vanuatu in 1980. It finished fourth in the [[OFC Nations Cup]] in [[Oceania Nations Cup 1973|1973]], [[Oceania Nations Cup 2000|2000]], and [[Oceania Nations Cup 2002|2002]]. In the [[Oceania Nations Cup 2004|2004 Oceania Nations Cup]], Vanuatu beat [[New Zealand national soccer team|New Zealand]] 4–2, preventing the regional powerhouse from making the final and, consequently, the running for the [[2006 FIFA World Cup]].

Vanuatu caused another shock in the 2007 [[South Pacific Games]] by knocking out the [[Solomon Islands national football team|Solomon Islands]] for bronze medal and also enable to enter the second stage of qualification for the OFC Nations Cup and consequently a chance with a playoff for the [[2010 FIFA World Cup]] held in South Africa. The last time they had won against Solomon Islands was back in 1998 and had since been on the receiving side of many losses save for one draw against them. In July 2008, Vanuatu faced two national teams from the Solomon Islands during the [[July 2008 first Wantok Cup|inaugural edition]] of the [[Wantok Cup]]. Vanuatu lost 1–2 to the Solomons' team A, but defeated their team B by two goals to one.

=== Recognition of the team efforts (2023–''present'') ===
In 2023, Vanuatu was invited for the [[2023 Intercontinental Cup (India)|2023 Intercontinental Cup]] tournament held in India which is the team first tournament outside [[Oceania]]. In the first match on 9 June 2023, they would face [[Lebanon national football team|Lebanon]] but suffered a 3–1 defeat. In the next match, Vanuatu conceded a late goal losing to host [[India men's national football team|India]] on a narrow 1–0 lost. On 15 June 2023, Vanuatu found themselves with a bright smiles as they would go on to win [[Mongolia national football team|Mongolia]] 1–0 thus earning them the 3 points and finishing in third place.

==== FIFA Series tournament ====
In 2024, [[FIFA]] invited Vanuatu to the [[2024 FIFA Series]] tournament held from 21 to 26 March 2024 in [[Jeddah]] where they would face [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] (CAF) and [[Brunei national football team|Brunei]] (AFC). On 21 March, Vanuatu faced their first ever opponent from the Africa continent, Guinea in a 6–0 lost.

=== First Oceanian final ===
Vanuatu progressed to the final of the [[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]] for the first time in [[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup|2024]] and finished as runners-up of the tournament on 30 June 2024 after losing to [[New Zealand men's national football team|New Zealand]] with a 3–0 scoreline.<ref>{{Cite web |title=New Zealand vs Vanuatu: live 29 june 2024. Team News, Preview, Lineups, Streaming, Sopcast {{!}} Soccer365 |url=https://soccer365.me/games/2096943/ |access-date=2024-07-08 |website=soccer365.me }}{{Dead link|date=October 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=New Zealand win record sixth OFC Nations Cup title |url=https://www.fifa.com/en/news/articles/ofc-nations-cup-new-zealand-champions |access-date=8 July 2024 |website=FIFA.com}}</ref>

Vanuatu then won the [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament|2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Vanuatu Stun PNG in Port Moresby to Claim First-Ever MSG Cup Crown |url=https://news.pngfacts.com/2025/11/vanuatu-stun-png-in-port-moresby-to.html |access-date=2025-11-23 |date=2025-11-23 |website=news.pngfacts.com }}</ref>

===Coaching history===

'''New Hebrides'''
* {{Flagicon|FRA}} [[Pierre Reichert]] (1973–1987)
'''Vanuatu'''
{{Div col|colwidth=17em}}
* {{Flagicon|ENG}} [[Terry O'Donnell (footballer)|Terry O'Donnell]] (1987–1993)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Saby Natonga]] (1996)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Alwyn Job]] (1998)
* {{Flagicon|URU}} [[Juan Carlos Buzzetti]] (2000–2004)
* {{Flagicon|ENG}} [[Joe Szekeres]] (2004–2007)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Robert Calvo]] (2007–2008)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Willian Malas]] (2008)
* {{Flagicon|URU}} [[Jorge Añón]] (2009)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Saby Natonga]] (2011–2012)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Percy Avock]] (2012–2015)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Moise Poida]] (2015–2017)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Etienne Mermer]] (2017–2018)
* {{Flagicon|NIR}} [[Paul Munster]] (2019)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} Etienne Mermer (2019–2022)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[George Amos (football coach)|George Amos]] (2022)
* {{Flagicon|URU}} [[Juan Carlos Buzzetti]] (2022)
* {{Flagicon|BRA}} [[Emerson Alcântara]] (2022)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} Etienne Mermer (2022–2023)
* {{Flagicon|BRA}} [[Emerson Alcântara]] (2023–2024)
* {{Flagicon|BRA}} [[Juliano Schmeling]] (2024–2025)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Joel Rarua]] (2025)
* {{Flagicon|VAN}} [[Richard Iwai]] (2025)
* {{flagicon|GER}} [[Lars Hopp]] (2025–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the national squad in the last twelve months.

{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|23 November 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Vanuatu |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/202/Vanuatu.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Vanuatu.''

|0
|1
|1
|1
|2
|- style="background:gold;"

{{col-end}}

}}

==Honours==
===Continental===
* '''[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2024 OFC Men's Nations Cup|2024]]

===Regional===
* '''[[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Silver medal (1): [[1971 South Pacific Games|1971]]
** {{Bronze3}} Bronze medal (3): [[1966 South Pacific Games|1966]], [[2003 South Pacific Games|2003]], [[2007 South Pacific Games|2007]]
* '''[[MSG Prime Minister's Cup|Melanesia Cup / MSG Prime Minister's Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (2)''': [[1990 Melanesia Cup|1990]], [[2025 MSG Prime Minister's Cup – Men's tournament|2025]]
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[1998 Melanesia Cup|1998]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (3): [[1988 Melanesia Cup|1988]], [[2000 Melanesia Cup|2000]], [[2023 MSG Prime Minister's Cup|2023]]
* '''[[Wantok Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2008 Wantok Cup|2008]]

===Summary===
{| class="wikitable" width=30% style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;"
|-
!Competition !!{{gold1}} !!{{silver2}} !!{{bronze3}} !!Total
|-
|align=left|[[OFC Men's Nations Cup|OFC Nations Cup]]
|0||1||0||1
|-
!Total!!0!!1!!0!!1
|}
